scope: MLB walkforward validation + WBC gate evidence + calibration comparison

interpretation.brier_vs_random: Brier=0.2487 vs random baseline=0.25; delta=-0.0013. Model is virtually indistinguishable from random probability assignment.
interpretation.hit_rate_below_50pct: ML hit rate 46.25% is BELOW the 50% break-even threshold. Model predictions are worse than a coin flip for moneyline betting over 1,626 bets.
interpretation.ou_roi_negative: OU ROI=-12.2% — model has NO predictive value for over/under totals.
interpretation.conclusion: The MLB walkforward model does not have a statistically meaningful edge over random chance.

interpretation.wbc_brier_good: 0.1415 is significantly better than MLB walkforward (0.2487) and beats random baseline
interpretation.small_sample_warning: N=40 games is too small for reliable inference. At N=40, a 95% CI on Brier is approximately ±0.03-0.05. The observed 0.1415 could plausibly range from 0.09 to 0.19.
interpretation.selection_bias_risk: WBC evaluable games may not be representative — games with sufficient prediction data may bias toward games with stable markets
interpretation.conclusion: WBC model appears better than random, but N=40 is insufficient to validate as production-ready.

interpretation.ece_acceptable: Both Platt ECE (3.52%) and Isotonic ECE (4.40%) are acceptable calibration errors (threshold typically 5%). Calibration itself is not the problem.
interpretation.brier_still_near_random: Even after calibration, Brier is 0.2484 (Platt) — still near-random.
interpretation.hit_rate_still_below_50: Platt hit_rate=46.44%, Isotonic hit_rate=48.03% — both below the 50% threshold. No profitable edge.
interpretation.isotonic_positive_roi_caveat: Isotonic shows ml_roi=+1.8%, but this is in-sample calibration — not a validated out-of-sample result. The underlying Brier=0.2504 is essentially random.
interpretation.conclusion: Calibration quality is acceptable but does not create profitability. The underlying model discriminative ability is the bottleneck.

model_contract_compliance.interpretation: Model outputs are missing 7 critical temporal metadata fields. M1/M5/M6/M13 gates failing at 0% suggests these are structural schema violations affecting ALL model outputs (not a subset). Without temporal metadata, auditing for data leakage or staleness is impossible.

gaps_identified.gap: Discriminative ability near random on large sample (MLB N=2188)
ML hit rate below 50% (worse than random for binary bet)
WBC sample too small for validation (N=40 evaluable games)

gap_summary.note: Even if CLV construction is fixed (addressing root cause #1), the underlying model would not generate positive CLV because the model has no demonstrated predictive advantage over the market. Both problems must be addressed independently.

DO_NOT_DO: Do NOT propose new model features without data leakage validation
Do NOT claim improved Brier without walkforward re-validation
Do NOT promote any challenger to production
research_directions_only: Feature engineering: investigate why hit_rate < 50% — potential feature leakage contaminating model
WBC-specific model: MLB model may not generalize to WBC. Separate WBC training with ≥1500 historical games required
MLB model retraining: current model may be underpowered, wrong features, or overfitted to noise
Contract compliance: add missing 7 temporal metadata fields to all model outputs
